description:
Aggregate and browse AI coding agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) in one place. Desktop app, self-hosted server, or Docker.

Codeg (Code Generation) is an enterprise-grade multi-agent coding workspace. It unifies local AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, Cline, etc.) in a desktop app, standalone server, or Docker container — enabling remote development from any browser — with conversation aggregation, parallel git worktree development, MCP/Skills management, chat channel interactions (Telegram, Lark, iLink, etc.), and integrated Git/file/terminal workflows.
- feat(acp): Meet Grok Build — the 11th built-in agent. xAI's Grok Build joins codeg as a first-class coding agent: sign in with your xAI API key, choose its model and reasoning effort, delegate sub-agents, and manage its MCP servers — with live and replayed sessions rendered exactly like every other agent.
- feat(science): A new Scientific Research skills mode. Thirteen curated, open-source research skills (from K-Dense-AI's scientific-agent-skills) now ship alongside the bundled expert and office skills. Install them to the shared library, enable them per agent in Settings, and start from the new "Scientific Research" tab on the new-conversation screen — localized in all 10 languages.
- feat(chat): The composer and your sent messages are now plain text. What you type, and the messages you've already sent, display as clean plain text with file and session references shown as compact badges — so your input reads exactly as written instead of being reflowed as Markdown. (Assistant replies are unchanged and still render Markdown.)
- fix(chat): Background tasks now settle correctly. A background task that's been collected or stopped is now marked as finished, so its indicator no longer lingers as if it were still running.
- fix(chat): The model picker stays open while you use its scrollbar. Dragging the scrollbar inside the model list no longer dismisses the picker.
